$comment: Synthetic adversarial fixture for the geo name resolver. Pinned outcomes that both .NET and TS resolvers MUST produce identically. Append-only — new rows added on regression discovery; rows are never removed.

countryCases.comment: exact display name
exact official name
alpha-3 code
case-insensitive after invariant casefold
lowercase free-form name resolves via invariant casefold
mixed-case free-form name resolves via invariant casefold

confusable pair member: Niger vs Nigeria

confusable pair member: Iran vs Iraq

confusable pair member: Slovakia vs Slovenia

confusable pair member: Sudan vs South Sudan (Pass-1 exact display name)
empty input — boundary validation
whitespace-only input — boundary validation
one character — too short for any pass that would hit
cascade exhausted with no match
Pass-1 exact: ISO 3166-1 numeric code 840 maps to US
Pass-1 exact via official-name field (historical English alias)
Pass-1 exact: ampersand-token expansion (' & ' to ' and ')
Pass-1 exact: 'and' form matches official-name field directly
Pass-1 exact via official-name field
Pass-1 exact display name with diacritic (ü)
locale-edge: NFD-strip turns ü into u so input without diacritic resolves identically
locale-edge: invariant casefold + NFD-strip handles Turkish dotted İ correctly (becomes 'i', not the tr-TR 'ı')
Pass-1 exact endonym for Germany
Pass-3 contains: 'burma' substring uniquely resolves to MM via DisplayName 'Myanmar (Burma)'
Pass-3 contains: 'vatican' substring resolves to VA (only VA's name fields contain 'vatican')
Pass-3 contains: 'macedonia' substring uniquely resolves to MK
Pass-3 ambiguity: 'korea' matches both KP (North Korea) and KR (South Korea) — fail-closed NotFound
Pass-3 ambiguity: 'republic' substring matches many official-name fields — fail-closed NotFound
Pass-2 ambiguity: startsWith 'unit' matches United States / United Kingdom / United Arab Emirates — fail-closed NotFound
Pass-2 ambiguity: startsWith 'united' matches multiple distinct countries — fail-closed NotFound
4-char input that misses Pass-1 / Pass-2; Pass-3 + Pass-4 are length-gated (require >=5 chars)
Pass-4 short-circuit pin: 4 chars, Pass-4 fuzzy would resolve to Iran but Pass-4 requires >=5 chars — fail-closed NotFound
Pass-4 Levenshtein distance-1 fuzzy: 'astralia' is one deletion from 'australia' (len 8, maxDistance = floor(8/5) = 1)
Pass-1 exact endonym (CJK Japanese kanji for Japan)
Pass-1 exact endonym (CJK simplified Chinese for People's Republic of China)
Pass-1 exact endonym (RTL Arabic for Saudi Arabia)

subdivisionCases.comment: exact display name within parent country
exact display name within parent country
parent-context discipline: Georgia is also a country (GE) but within US scope resolves to GA
parent-context discipline: no Georgia subdivision in Canada
no match in parent — Ottawa is a Canadian city, not a US subdivision
parent-context: 'Washington' matches state WA via DisplayName; DC carries 'District of Columbia' so no ambiguity
Pass-3 ambiguity: 'carolina' matches both North Carolina (US-NC) and South Carolina (US-SC) — fail-closed NotFound
exact-match short-circuits Pass-3: full DisplayName beats the 'Carolina'-only ambiguity
exact-match short-circuits Pass-3: full DisplayName beats the 'Carolina'-only ambiguity
Pass-4 Levenshtein distance-1: one insertion away from 'california' (len 9, maxDistance = floor(9/5) = 1)
Pass-4 Levenshtein distance-1: one deletion away from 'texas' (len 6, maxDistance = floor(6/5) = 1)
Pass-4 short-circuit pin: 4 chars — Pass-3 + Pass-4 require >=5 chars and Pass-2 startsWith 'texs' has no match — fail-closed NotFound
Pass-1 exact via DisplayName with diacritic (ã)
NFD normalization: input without diacritic resolves identically to São Paulo
Pass-1 exact via DisplayName with diacritic (Î)
NFD normalization: input without diacritic resolves identically to Île-de-France
